topic: Cron patterns -- skip-if-running, last-run persistence, restart catchup

claims.text: Skip-if-running is implemented via active-jobs.ts: a process-global Set<string> (CronActiveJobState) keyed by Symbol.for('openclaw.cron.activeJobs') + resolveGlobalSingleton(). markCronJobActive(jobId), clearCronJobActive(jobId), isCronJobActive(jobId). The bug in issue #79196 (cron tasks marked as lost after restart) is because this Set is in-memory only -- NOT persisted to disk. It's a known limitation.
claims.worktrain_adaptation: WorkTrain's PollingScheduler already has implicit skip-if-running via per-trigger KeyedAsyncQueue serialization. But the Set-based pattern from OpenClaw is useful for the daemon's startup recovery: WorkTrain's DAEMON_SESSIONS_DIR sidecars serve the same purpose but need cleanup on restart. The lesson from OpenClaw's bug: always persist the active-set before the process can crash, not just in-memory.

claims.text: CronRunLogEntry is an append-only JSONL per job (one file per jobId, not one global file): resolveCronRunLogPath({ storePath, jobId }) returns <runsDir>/<safeJobId>.jsonl. Path is validated with isPathInside(runsDir, resolvedPath) to prevent traversal. File is chmod 0o600.
claims.worktrain_adaptation: WorkTrain's execution-stats.jsonl is one global file. Per-session files (like the existing session sidecars) are a better pattern. The chmod 0o600 + isPathInside path validation is directly adoptable for any WorkTrain file writes in ~/.workrail/.

claims.text: On startup, cron ops.ts marks any job with state.runningAtMs set as 'interrupted' (not as crashed/failed). It sets job.state.lastRunStatus = 'error', consecutiveErrors++, and nextRunAtMs = undefined. One-shot 'at' jobs are disabled. Then reschedules from that point. This is the 'interrupted startup run' pattern -- distinct from a normal failure.
claims.worktrain_adaptation: WorkTrain's startup recovery distinguishes 0-steps (discard) vs 1+-steps (resume). OpenClaw's interrupted-startup-run pattern adds a third category: 'interrupted mid-execution' -- mark the outcome, increment error count, and re-schedule rather than either resuming or discarding. Useful when a session is too far along to discard but not worth resuming.

claims.text: LRU cache for cron expression evaluation: Map<string, Cron> with max size 512. Key = `${timezone}\u0000${expr}`. On cache miss, creates new Cron(); on cache hit, delete+re-insert to move to end for LRU eviction. O(1) amortized.
claims.worktrain_adaptation: WorkTrain's PollingScheduler doesn't cache computed next-run times. Not urgent, but the null-byte-separator key pattern (timezone + expr) for LRU is a clean idiom to adopt whenever WorkTrain starts computing next-poll times.
